mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and supports valuation expansion, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy remains easy.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 20-day forward returns are not above unconditional returns, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in CPIAUCSL inflation forces tighter policy expectations and higher real yields, which compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the inflation acceleration is observed.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of inflation-acceleration episodes, or if the signal fails when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with declining PAYEMS marks labor-market deterioration, reducing earnings expectations and increasing recession probability, which lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS deterioration does not improve 60-day downside hit rate versus either series alone, or if positive 60-day returns dominate after labor deterioration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly growth in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer demand and revenue support for listed firms, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when retail sales momentum is clearly positive.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth above 1.0% does not produce above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO combined with stable or falling CPIAUCSL reflects stronger real activity without inflation pressure, which supports margins and risk appetite and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes without concurrent CPIAUCSL restraint do not outperform single-series benchmarks, or if 20-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-signal periods.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts collapse signals weakening construction demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical activity, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days after HOUST falls sharply.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses significance in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS grows while UNRATE is flat or falling, labor income and consumption support remain intact, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved risk sentiment.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not outperform either component alone on 5-day returns, or if positive returns do not exceed 55% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% together with inflation pressure from CPIAUCSL compresses equity valuation multiples and raises recession risk, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes with CPIAUCSL pressure do not produce lower 20-day returns than low-rate episodes, or if the joint signal fails to stay below a 45% positive-return hit rate.
